name: has (from lodash)
description: A source-faithful extracted bundle of lodash's has method and its required helpers, cut from the full lodash package instead of the standalone lodash.has package.
tagline: Extract lodash has from the monolith with helpers included.
summary: This page ships an extracted bundle from the full lodash package. The point is not to use the modular lodash.has package, but to prove that path-existence checks can be cut out of the monolith with their helper chain intact.
nativeAlternative: Direct property checks work for shallow cases, but dynamic paths and lodash-style semantics make a reusable extracted helper more attractive.

variants.label: Normalized
variants.note: Rule-based normalization after extraction. The extraction defines the raw bundle; normalization only changes the packaging shape.

variants.label: Raw
variants.note: Source-faithful extracted bundle from the lodash package, with local helper modules inlined and module boundaries removed.

note: This snippet is a monolith extraction experiment. It differs from the modular lodash.has package because the raw source is assembled from the full lodash package's internal module graph.
